sink_impls.notes: Production standard (grep-style) output. impl Sink block ~line 807; matched() at 810, begin() at 903, finish() at 915. Instantiated via Standard::sink() / Standard::sink_with_path().
Production JSON Lines output. impl Sink block ~line 684; matched() at 687, begin() at 775, finish() at 792. Instantiated via JSON::sink() / JSON::sink_with_path().
Production summary (count/path-only) output. impl Sink block ~line 646; matched() at 649, begin() at 708, finish() at 726. Instantiated via Summary::sink() / Summary::sink_with_path().
Closure-based sink for UTF-8 matched lines. Defined in an inline test/example submodule (imports via `use super::*`). Only implements matched(); all other Sink methods use defaults.
Closure-based sink with lossy UTF-8 conversion. Same inline submodule as UTF8. Only implements matched().
Closure-based raw-bytes sink. Same inline submodule as UTF8. Only implements matched().
Blanket impl enabling mutable references to any Sink to also satisfy Sink. matched() body at line 229.
Blanket impl enabling boxed Sink trait objects. matched() body at line 282.

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search
call_chain.notes: Entry point. Checks should_decompress / should_preprocess, delegates to search_preprocessor, search_decompress, or search_path (method).

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_path (method)
call_chain.notes: Opens the file at the given Path, then calls the free function search_path.

call_chain.name: search_path (free fn) — dispatch point
call_chain.notes: Matches on Printer<W> enum (Standard/Summary/JSON), calls .sink_with_path() to instantiate the concrete Sink, then calls Searcher::search_path with it.

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_reader (method)
call_chain.notes: Alternative path for piped/preprocessed input. Calls the free function search_reader.

call_chain.name: search_reader (free fn) — dispatch point
call_chain.notes: Matches on Printer<W> enum to instantiate StandardSink / JSONSink / SummarySink via .sink_with_path(), then calls Searcher::search_reader with the concrete Sink.

call_chain.name: Searcher::search_reader
call_chain.notes: Core searcher method. Accepts any S: Sink, M: Matcher, R: Read. Handles transcoding/encoding, delegates into glue layer (ReadByLine / MultiLine) which drives all Sink callbacks (begin, matched, context, finish).

change_impact_map.change: CREATE. Define CSV<W>, CSVBuilder, and CSVSink<'p,'s,M,W> structs. Implement CSVBuilder::build(wtr: W) -> CSV<W>; CSV::sink() / CSV::sink_with_path() -> CSVSink; impl Sink for CSVSink with at minimum matched(), begin(), and finish(). Model after crates/printer/src/json.rs.
ADD `mod csv;` module declaration and re-export `pub use crate::csv::{CSV, CSVBuilder, CSVSink};` alongside the existing Standard/Summary/JSON re-exports.
ADD `Csv(grep::printer::CSV<W>)` variant to the Printer<W> enum.
ADD match arm for Printer::Csv in the free fn search_path dispatch block: instantiate CSVSink via .sink_with_path(), pass to searcher.search_path().
ADD match arm for Printer::Csv in the free fn search_reader dispatch block: instantiate CSVSink via .sink_with_path(), pass to searcher.search_reader().
ADD arm `Printer::Csv(ref mut p) => p.get_mut()` to SearchWorker::get_mut() so the inner writer remains accessible.
ADD Printer::Csv construction branch in the printer-selection logic, triggered when the CSV output format flag is set. Locate existing Standard/Summary/JSON branches and add a parallel Csv branch.
ADD --output-format=csv (or --csv) flag definition, wiring it to select Printer::Csv in main.rs.
ADD csv-encoding dependency (e.g., the `csv` crate) if RFC 4180 escaping is required; omit if encoding is handled manually with the existing CounterWriter infrastructure.
